context: 3 tasks were scheduled for 3/30 but the day included 4 formal meetings plus 3 instant meetings with Ben/Collins. Only 5 min free time available.
decision: No scheduled tasks were completed. Meetings were effectively prioritized over all task work.
reasoning: With 5 min free time and back-to-back calls, there was no viable window to start any of the 3 scheduled tasks. Implicit decision to let meetings run rather than block time for deep work.

context: Investor Count Stamping QA (due 2026-03-23) and HubSpot Workflows Review (due 2026-03-27) both have passed due dates and were carried into the 3/30 schedule but not completed.
decision: Both overdue tasks deferred again -- 9 and 4 days overdue respectively as of 3/31.
reasoning: Meeting load prevented completion. Recurring deferral across multiple days suggests tasks are either underestimated, lower priority in practice than in planning, or need to be broken into smaller chunks.

context: Ben Mizes initiated 2 instant meetings (3:39 PM and another) after SOW Kickoff, with an instant meeting with Collins also at 3:14 PM. These were unplanned.
decision: Drake accepted all instant meetings, consuming afternoon task time.
reasoning: Instant meetings with CEO are implicitly higher priority than solo task work. SOW Kickoff likely generated immediate follow-ups needing real-time resolution.
